relations.note: Specifically refines contrast limitations with a hard restriction on using gold/sunshine tones over white backgrounds.

quotes: Test contrast levels (webaim.com or equivalent) for every text/background pair. [GENERAL minimums: WCAG AA — 4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text ≥24px or 19px bold.]
Known-safe pairs: Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.

rules.intent: Maintain strict visual accessibility compliance according to WCAG AA parameters.
rules.rule_text: Test contrast levels for every text/background pair to ensure compliance with WCAG AA standards (minimums of 4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text >=24px or 19px bold). Approved safe pairs include using Brand Blue, Deep Blue, Dark Navy, or Graphite text on White, Light Blue, or Golden Sand backgrounds, as well as White text on Brand Blue, Deep Blue, or Dark Navy backgrounds.

quotes: Avoid: Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast) — golds are for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

rules.intent: Prevent unreadable low-contrast text on bright backgrounds.
rules.rule_text: Do not use Gold (color.primary.gold = #ffc60a) or Sunshine (color.primary.sunshine = #faa31b) text on a White background as they fail standard contrast minimums. These colors are reserved for solid container surfaces, horizontal rules, and large display elements only.

quotes: Don't rely on color alone to convey meaning — pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

rules.intent: Ensure content remains understandable for colorblind and visually impaired users.
rules.rule_text: Do not rely on color alone to convey key meaning or feedback. Color changes must be paired with secondary indicators such as icons, structural depth, explicit copy, or other visual signals.
